pattern: Sweeping right to left, each element is split into as few pieces as possible whose largest part stays under the running ceiling.
hints: The last element can never be split usefully, so process from the right with it as the upper bound.
To break a value v into parts each at most the current limit, the minimum number of parts is ceil(v / limit).
When split into p parts the largest is minimized at floor(v / p); that floor becomes the new limit for the element to its left, and you add p - 1 operations.

edge_inputs.description: a middle peak must be broken to fit both neighbours
value not evenly divisible by required part count

stress.description: moderate array with swings to exercise the ceiling arithmetic without exploding the linear-search oracle
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 300))
    nums = [rng.randint(1, 500) for _ in range(n)]
    return [nums]
